solo.title: Solo Creator
solo.desc: You work best alone and own the whole thing end to end. Autonomy is your fuel; coordination is your cost. You thrive when the scope can be carried by one person and you stay clear of politics.
solo.risks: You become a bottleneck as the work grows; you build the only thing that cannot survive your absence.

dyad.title: Dyad Partner
dyad.desc: You work best with one trusted partner in a deep partnership. Two aligned people carry more than one and almost as fast. Your fuel is trust; your risk is fragility if the dyad breaks.
dyad.risks: Single point of failure: if the partnership ruptures, the venture can fracture with it.

team.title: Team Player
team.desc: You thrive in a structured small team of three to seven with clear roles. Coordination energizes you and you make others reliable. Your fuel is collective momentum.
team.risks: You may wait for consensus when no one is ready to decide; structure can become a comfort zone.

weaver.title: Community Weaver
weaver.desc: You build trust and connections across groups and enable a wider network to do together what none could alone. Coordination at scale is your gift; process overhead is your tax.
weaver.risks: Harmony can become conflict-avoidance; you may dilute accountability in the name of inclusion.

mentor.title: Mentor / Catalyst
mentor.desc: You enable others to do their best work — coaching rather than doing. Your impact multiples through people you develop. Your fuel is their growth; your cost is staying out of the driver seat.
mentor.risks: You may manage instead of build when the moment needs your own hands; impact is secondhand.

flatNote: Your answers are fairly evenly spread. You are a genuine generalist collaborator — able to shift between solo, pair, team, and community modes depending on the situation. This is valuable, but it means you should name your default intentionally when a venture needs a clear collaboration shape.
teamConstellation: Bring three or more colleagues and compare results to see which species your team has in surplus and which are absent.

shareTexts.invite: What is your collaboration species — solo, dyad, team, weaver, or mentor?
shareTexts.minimal: My collaboration species is {primary}. What is yours?
shareTexts.full:
My collaboration species is {primary}, with {secondary} as a close second.
What is yours?

recommendation.label: Outfinity initiative
recommendation.title: Explorer Circle
recommendation.text: Explorer Circle is a community for builders mapping how they actually work together and finding collaborators with complementary species. You do not need to share your result to participate.
recommendation.note: Opening Explorer Circle takes you to Substack. Your result is not transmitted.
